description: AI Physique Analysis v2 - Upgrade to IFBB/NPC rules, category selector, posing mode, structured scores, and evolution chart

userStories.title: Extend PhysiqueCheckIn type and store with v2 fields
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to extend the store types to support target category, competition timeline, scores, and posing mode.
acceptanceCriteria: PhysiqueCheckIn type gains `targetCategory: 'mens_physique' | 'classic_physique' | 'bodybuilding' | 'undecided'` (required field)
PhysiqueCheckIn type gains `weeksToCompetition?: number` (optional)
PhysiqueCheckIn type gains `scores?: { overallConditioning?: number; stageReadiness?: number; vTaper?: number }` (optional)
PhysiqueCheckIn.mode union updated to `'full' | 'comparative' | 'quick' | 'posing'`
MODE_LABELS updated to include `posing: 'Posing'`
Store gains `lastCategory: 'mens_physique' | 'classic_physique' | 'bodybuilding' | 'undecided'` field in state (persisted), defaults to 'undecided'
Store gains `setLastCategory(category)` action that updates lastCategory
addCheckIn action also calls setLastCategory with the check-in's targetCategory
Typecheck passes (`npx tsc --noEmit`)

userStories.title: Replace system prompt with v2 version
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to update the system prompt in services/physiqueAnalysis.ts with the full v2 prompt containing IFBB/NPC rules, weight classes, mandatory poses, judging criteria, and 7-section protocol.
acceptanceCriteria: SYSTEM_PROMPT in services/physiqueAnalysis.ts is replaced with the full v2 prompt from ai-physique-analysis-prompt-v2.md (the content inside the first ``` code block under ## System Prompt)
Athlete context variables (ATHLETE_NAME, ATHLETE_AGE, ATHLETE_HEIGHT, ATHLETE_PHASE) are still interpolated via template literals

The prompt includes: eligible categories with weight/height limits, mandatory poses per category, official judging criteria, common scoring errors, and all 7 analysis sections (including Posing & Apresentação and Stage Readiness & Timeline)

Add instruction at end of system prompt: 'Ao final da sua análise, SEMPRE inclua um bloco JSON com scores extraídos no formato: ```json\n{"overallConditioning": <1-10>, "stageReadiness": <0-100>, "vTaper": <1-10>}\n```'
max_tokens increased from 4096 to 8192
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Update AnalysisContext and user prompt templates to v2 format
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to update the prompt builder functions to include category, competition timeline, and match the v2 template format.
acceptanceCriteria: AnalysisContext type gains `targetCategory: 'mens_physique' | 'classic_physique' | 'bodybuilding' | 'undecided'` and `weeksToCompetition?: number`
Add CATEGORY_LABELS map: { mens_physique: "Men's Physique", classic_physique: "Classic Physique", bodybuilding: "Bodybuilding", undecided: "A definir (sugira)" }
buildFullPrompt includes: peso atual, peso anterior with delta, categoria alvo (translated label), semanas para competição (or 'sem data definida'), observações
buildComparativePrompt includes all of the above plus instruction to 'Analise o progresso seguindo o protocolo completo com ênfase no comparativo e na recomendação de categoria'
buildQuickPrompt includes: peso, categoria alvo, and 'avaliação rápida' instruction

New buildPosingPrompt function: includes categoria alvo and instruction 'Avalie cada pose: execução, o que favorece, o que expõe, como melhorar. Sugira a melhor favorite classic pose baseado no meu físico.'
analyzePhysique function signature updated to accept 'posing' mode and calls buildPosingPrompt when mode is 'posing'
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Extract structured scores from API response
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to parse the JSON scores block from the analysis response and return it separately.
acceptanceCriteria: analyzePhysique return type changes from `string` to `{ analysis: string; scores?: { overallConditioning?: number; stageReadiness?: number; vTaper?: number } }`
After receiving API response text, use regex to find ```json...``` block at the end
Parse the JSON block and extract overallConditioning, stageReadiness, vTaper as numbers
Strip the JSON block (including the ``` fences) from the analysis markdown text
If JSON parsing fails or block not found, scores is undefined (non-fatal, no error thrown)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add category selector and weeks-to-competition to check-in form
userStories.description: As a user, I want to select my target competition category and optionally enter weeks to competition on the check-in form.
acceptanceCriteria: Category picker appears below the mode selector row, as a row of 4 pill buttons: 'Physique', 'Classic', 'Bodybuilding', 'A definir'
Category picker defaults to lastCategory from usePhysiqueStore
Selected pill uses amber accent background (same pattern as mode selector)
Optional 'Semanas para competição' number input field appears below category picker, keyboardType 'number-pad', placeholder 'Ex: 12'
Both values passed to analyzePhysique context on submit
Selected category saved to store via setLastCategory on submit
Check-in saved with targetCategory and weeksToCompetition fields
new-checkin.tsx handles the new { analysis, scores } return type from analyzePhysique — saves scores to the check-in
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add posing evaluation mode with category-specific photo slots
userStories.description: As a user, I want a 'Posing' mode with labeled photo slots matching the mandatory poses for my selected category.
acceptanceCriteria: Mode selector shows 3 options: 'Completa', 'Quick', 'Posing'
When 'Posing' is selected, category picker cannot be 'A definir' — if it is, show inline hint text 'Selecione uma categoria para o modo Posing' and disable submit
PhotoSlots component accepts an optional `labels` prop (string array) to override the default LABELS constant

When mode is 'posing', photo slot labels change based on targetCategory: Men's Physique: ['Front Pose', 'Side Esquerdo', 'Back Pose', 'Side Direito'] (4 slots). Classic Physique: ['Front Double Biceps', 'Side Chest', 'Back Double Biceps', 'Abs & Thighs', 'Favorite Classic'] (5 slots). Bodybuilding: ['Front Double Biceps', 'Front Lat Spread', 'Side Chest', 'Back Double Biceps', 'Back Lat Spread', 'Side Triceps', 'Abs & Thighs', 'Most Muscular'] (8 slots)
PhotoSlots visibleSlots updates to match the number of labels for the selected category in posing mode
photos state array size accommodates up to 8 slots (expand from 4)
Posing mode uses 'posing' as effectiveMode (no auto-comparative detection)
Timeout extended to 90000ms when mode is 'posing' (to handle 5-8 photos)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Display structured scores on result screen
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see my conditioning, stage readiness, and v-taper scores displayed visually on the analysis result.
acceptanceCriteria: If checkIn.scores exists and has at least one defined value, a scores section appears between photo thumbnails and analysis markdown
Three score cards in a horizontal row with equal width
Card 1: 'Condicionamento' label, score value out of 10 (e.g. '7/10'), amber accent text for the number
Card 2: 'Stage Ready' label, score value as percentage (e.g. '65%'), with a small horizontal progress bar (amber fill on dark bg)
Card 3: 'V-Taper' label, score value out of 10 (e.g. '8/10'), amber accent text for the number
Cards use bg-elevated background, rounded corners (theme.radius.lg), padding 12
If a specific score is undefined, that card shows '--' instead of a number
If checkIn.scores is undefined or all values are undefined, the entire section is hidden
Result screen header also shows targetCategory as a badge (e.g. 'Classic Physique') next to the mode badge, if defined
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add stage readiness evolution chart to Progresso index
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a line chart showing my stage readiness score progression across weeks.
acceptanceCriteria: Install react-native-gifted-charts: `npm install react-native-gifted-charts react-native-linear-gradient react-native-svg --legacy-peer-deps`
Create components/physique/EvolutionChart.tsx component
Chart data sourced from all check-ins that have scores.stageReadiness defined, ordered by week ascending
X-axis shows week numbers, Y-axis shows 0-100% range
Line chart with amber accent color line, data points as small filled circles
Dark theme: chart background transparent, axis labels use theme.colors.text.muted, grid lines subtle
Chart appears on progresso index screen above the check-in list cards
Chart only renders when 2 or more check-ins have stageReadiness scores
Chart has a title label 'Stage Readiness' above it in footnote style
Typecheck passes
